summary: Matched most of Yoshi's ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_IASA functions in ftYs_SpecialN.c, leaving ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_IASA as the known remaining larger function. The PR added typed motion-var views for Yoshi specialn, specials, and specialhi state, added x48/x4C fields to ftYoshiAttributes, corrected SpecialAirHi collision code to use specialhi vars, and gave ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC a concrete prototype so the matched IASA functions could call it cleanly.
what_changed: The new IASA functions check whether fp->mv.ys.specials.x0 is less than attributes->x48 - attributes->x4C and whether B is held via fp->input.x668 & HSD_PAD_B.
The matched loop functions call ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC(gobj, mode, 0x440012, ftYs_Init_804D9A70), using mode 0 for Loop_1 and mode 1 for Loop_2/Loop_3.
Added global f32 ftYs_Init_804D9A70 = 0.0f for the float argument passed into ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC.
Changed ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC declaration from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC(ftYs_GObj* gobj, s32, s32, f32).
Added ftYoshiAttributes fields x48 and x4C and shrank the following filler from 0xCA to 0xC2 to preserve struct layout.
Reworked ftYoshi_SpecialNVars from a nested union/byte-bitfield shape into direct bitfields x0_b0 through x0_b3 at fp+2340, followed by x4, padding x8, and x30.
Added separate motion-var structs for ftYoshi_SpecialSVars and ftYoshi_SpecialHiVars, each exposing x0 at fp+2340.
Updated existing specialn bitfield references from fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0.x0_bytes.x0_b0 to fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0_b0.
Corrected ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Coll to test fp->mv.ys.specialhi.x0 instead of fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0.x0.
smart_moves: Splitting Yoshi motion vars by move family made the code express intent better: SpecialAirS loop logic uses mv.ys.specials, SpecialHi collision uses mv.ys.specialhi, and neutral-special flag code remains under mv.ys.specialn.
The concrete prototype for ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC removed an UNK_PARAMS call barrier and documented the observed argument pattern used by the newly matched IASA functions.
Adding x48 and x4C to ftYoshiAttributes allowed the IASA threshold expression to be written as normal dat_attrs field access rather than M2C_FIELD-style offset access.
The reviewer-observed mismatch between function names and attr/motion-var selection was addressed in the final diff by using fp->mv.ys.specials.x0 in the SpecialAirS loop implementations.
Leaving ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_IASA as a stub was explicitly scoped in the PR body because it appeared to be a larger remaining function.
decomp_lessons: For fighter motion variables stored in a shared union, create per-move structs once callsites show that the same fp+2340 storage has different meanings for SpecialN, SpecialS, and SpecialHi.
Function-name context can be useful review evidence: SpecialAirS functions should be checked for use of specials vars/attrs rather than blindly reusing nearby specialn fields.

When matching IASA functions, look for simple input-gated transition patterns: compare a move timer/counter against an attribute-derived threshold, test HSD_PAD_B, then call the move transition helper with the expected state flags and blend/start-frame float.
Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes can unlock cleaner matching and makes future callsite recovery easier.
When adding fields to dat_attrs, adjust filler size carefully so later known offsets such as x114, x118, and x11C remain aligned with the original layout.
Direct named bitfields can be preferable to nested anonymous-looking union wrappers when the code only needs flag access and the old representation makes callsites noisy.
naming_conventions: Yoshi side-special motion vars were named ftYoshi_SpecialSVars and accessed as fp->mv.ys.specials.
Yoshi up-special motion vars were named ftYoshi_SpecialHiVars and accessed as fp->mv.ys.specialhi.
Yoshi neutral-special motion vars remained ftYoshi_SpecialNVars and accessed as fp->mv.ys.specialn.
Bitfields formerly under x0.x0_bytes were flattened to x0_b0, x0_b1, x0_b2, and x0_b3.
Unknown Yoshi attributes were kept as offset-style names x48 and x4C pending semantic identification.
The newly added float global followed the existing symbol-address naming style ftYs_Init_804D9A70.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use temporary locals for attribute fields when needed for matching, as in x48, x4C, and difference = x48 - x4C in ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_1_IASA.
For similar sibling IASA functions, compare whether the compiler matches with an explicit difference local versus an inline attributes->x48 - attributes->x4C expression.
Model shared fighter motion-var storage as union members for different Yoshi specials so the same fp+2340 offset can decompile to move-specific names without offset hacks.
Use the HSD_PAD_B mask against fp->input.x668 for B-button IASA checks.
Convert known helper calls from UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures once argument types are observed repeatedly across matched functions.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Based on the function names, shouldn't these be using `specials` attrs?
review_feedback.outcome: The final diff uses fp->mv.ys.specials.x0 in the SpecialAirS loop functions. The exact resolution for whether separate `specials` dat_attrs should exist is not fully documented in the provided slice.

review_feedback.author: Gelatart
review_feedback.comment: Yoshi doesn't seem to have specials attributes, should we make some? Or are they somewhere else?
review_feedback.outcome: The PR added motion-var structs for specials and specialhi, while the attribute fields used by the loop functions remained on ftYoshiAttributes as x48 and x4C.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match ftYs_SpecialAirSLoop_0_IASA.
Search for later renames or semantic identification of ftYoshiAttributes x48 and x4C.
Check whether ftYs_Init_804D9A70 was later renamed or moved to a generated symbol/header.
Search for later changes to ftYoshi_SpecialSVars and ftYoshi_SpecialHiVars to confirm the motion-var layout.
Inspect follow-up PRs touching ftYs_SpecialS_8012F0DC for a more semantic function name or argument names.
